method: React canonical (react-dom/server renderToStaticMarkup on @cloudflare/kumo) vs native Svelte (svelte/compiler generate:server + svelte/server render on generated/libraries/svelte/components), both pages load kumo-standalone.css at an identical fixed origin, screenshotted headless via Playwright Chrome-for-Testing (--headless=new --screenshot, device-scale-factor=1, forced white bg), diffed with pixelmatch (threshold=0.1, includeAA=true). % is measured over the component content bounding box (not the whole viewport). flag threshold = 1% of bbox pixels.
pct_basis: component content bounding box (non-white union of both renders)
notes: Checkbox defect (native has no checkmark + fill-only box) is caught: the checkmark glyph region is a solid block of mismatched pixels in checkbox.diff.png.
Switch defect (native: no blue track, tiny thumb parked left, wrong size h-5/w-10 vs React h-4.5/w-9) is caught massively: color + position + size all differ.
Button (known-good) is byte-identical HTML modulo React wrapper span; renders pixel-identical -> ~0% diff -> no false positive.

AA/font-noise sensitivity: because BOTH sides use the identical kumo-standalone.css, same fallback font, forced white background, device-scale-factor=1 and identical fixed origin, text anti-aliasing is deterministic and cancels out (button 0%). The pixel method is only trustworthy when layout/origin is pinned identically; it CANNOT distinguish a genuine 1px layout shift from a defect, so it flags size differences (switch) as defects too. It measures literal look, not intent.

Limitation: method requires identical positioning + same CSS; it is a strong FINAL 1:1 confirmation but blind to WHY (missing element vs recolor vs shift) — pair with DOM/computed-style diff for diagnosis.
